steps.title: Calculate the retail price
steps.content:
The retail price is 260% of the wholesale price.

$$\text{Retail price} = 2.60 \times 7.00 = \$18.20$$

steps.title: Apply the discount using the complement percentage
steps.content: The key to speed: 75% off means the customer pays the remaining 25% of the retail price. Don't calculate the discount amount separately—multiply the retail price by 0.25 directly. $$\text{Discounted price} = 0.25 \times 18.20 = \$4.55$$
